scope: Phase-1 code quality on the assembled whole — cross-cutting concerns
head: fc3f0d1
summary: Workspace is in solid shape: clippy -D warnings clean across workspace+all-targets, fmt clean, zero actual todo!() or unimplemented!() macros left in library code, no unused imports/dead-code compile warnings, error enums all thiserror-derived with lowercase messages, test layout consistent (integration tests via `tests/` per crate; benten-engine uses an aggregator for build-time economy — documented intent), FFI boundary shows proper validation on every JSON decode path. The findings below are nearly all housekeeping: stale doc-comments and stale `#[allow]` reasons that reference an R3/red-phase state the code has already moved past, plus a broken intra-doc link and undocumented public items on the EngineBuilder/Engine surface. None of these block the gate — they are a cleanup pass.

pipeline_check.cargo_fmt_check: PASS (per prior gate record; not re-run here)

findings.title: Broken intra-doc link `benten_graph::RedbBackend` — only warning emitted by cargo doc
findings.description: The `GrantReader` trait doc says `the engine implements this trait against its own [`benten_graph::RedbBackend`]`. benten-caps intentionally has no regular dep on benten-graph (it's dev-dep only, per layering rule stated in the doc itself), so rustdoc cannot resolve the link. This is the ONLY warning `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps` emits across all six crates.
findings.recommendation: Replace the rustdoc-link form `[`benten_graph::RedbBackend`]` with backtick-literal `` `benten_graph::RedbBackend` `` (no square brackets). The prose explanation makes the intent clear without needing the resolvable link, and the layering comment directly above the link already explains why benten-caps can't depend on benten-graph.

findings.title: Many pub items on EngineBuilder/Engine lack doc comments — 125 missing-doc sites in benten-engine alone
findings.description:
Running `RUSTDOCFLAGS='-D missing_docs' cargo doc --no-deps --lib -p benten-engine` reports 125 undocumented pub items. Sampling the list: every EngineBuilder fluent method (`production`, `without_ivm`, `without_caps`, `without_versioning`, `with_test_ivm_budget`, `ivm_max_work_per_update`) and every Phase-2 stub method (`create_anchor`, `append_version`, `read_current_version`, `walk_versions`, `schedule_revocation_at_iteration`) lacks docs. The builder methods are the discoverability entry point for library users — missing docs here shows up directly in the published crate page.

Per-crate counts with `-D missing_docs`: benten-core 31, benten-graph 0, benten-ivm 5, benten-caps 2, benten-eval 125, benten-engine 69. (235 total across the six library crates.)
findings.recommendation: Phase-1 targeted pass: document all EngineBuilder methods (one-line `/// Enable X ...` suffices on a fluent setter) and all Phase-2 stub methods (`/// Returns `EngineError::NotImplemented`; scheduled for Phase 2.` is adequate). Then add `#![warn(missing_docs)]` to each crate's `lib.rs` so further drift is visible in CI. Gate full enforcement (`deny(missing_docs)`) on a follow-up cleanup pass, not this cycle.

findings.title: Stale `#![allow(clippy::todo)]` reason references removed `todo!()` calls
findings.description: `#![allow(clippy::todo, reason = "Phase 1 leaves some per-view methods as todo!() for later groups")]` but there are zero `todo!()` calls left in `crates/benten-ivm/src/**`. The allow is a no-op; the reason is factually wrong.
findings.recommendation: Delete the `#![allow(clippy::todo, ...)]` attribute entirely. The workspace-wide `todo = "allow"` already covers edge cases and this file-level allow is redundant.

findings.title: Stale `#![allow(clippy::todo)]` reason claims `R3 red-phase stubs; R5 removes todos` — but R5 has completed
findings.description: No `todo!()` macro calls exist in `crates/benten-eval/src/**`. The allow is redundant (workspace allows `todo`) and the reason is factually wrong post-R5.
findings.recommendation: Delete line 11. Leave the `result_large_err` and `too_many_lines` allows (lines 12-19) — those reasons remain valid.

findings.title: Stale `#![allow(clippy::todo)]` reason `Phase 1 stubs cleared as G2-B/G3/G5/G6 land` — all those groups have shipped
findings.description: G2-B, G3, G5, G6 are all complete. No `todo!()` calls remain in benten-graph/src. The file-level allow is a no-op and the reason text is historical.

findings.title: Module doc still reads `Operation primitives + evaluator (Phase 1 stubs)` — the evaluator is implemented
findings.description: Header comment `R3 stub scaffold — R5 implementation lands in Phase 1 proper.` is stale. benten-eval ships 8 executing primitives + full registration-time invariant validation.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite header to describe the shipped surface: 12 primitive types defined, 8 executable primitives (READ/WRITE/TRANSFORM/BRANCH/ITERATE/CALL/RESPOND/EMIT), invariants 1-6/9/10/12 enforced, transform expression evaluator + grammar, diag feature for Mermaid+trace. Note the 4 deferred primitives explicitly.

findings.title: Published-crate descriptions still say `(Phase 1 stub)`
findings.description: `description = "Capability policy hook + NoAuthBackend for the Benten graph engine (Phase 1 stub)."` and `description = "Operation primitives and evaluator for the Benten graph engine (Phase 1 stub)."` Both crates are fully implemented for Phase 1.
findings.recommendation: Drop the `(Phase 1 stub)` tail from both `description` fields. This surfaces on crates.io / `cargo search` output and docs.rs crate headers.

findings.title: STUB_MARKER constants + TODO(phase-1-cleanup, G8) still in place — the cleanup was scheduled for G8 and G8 is done
findings.description:
The plan was explicitly `TODO(phase-1-cleanup, G8): retire these three const _: assertions together with the STUB_MARKER constants in benten-caps / benten-eval / benten-ivm once those crates are no longer stub-phase`. G8 has shipped. The three marker constants are now just unused-pub-const smoke that looks confusing to readers.

Separately: the two phase-1 marker strings are inconsistent — `benten-caps::phase-1` and `benten-ivm::phase-1` but `benten-eval::stub`. No consumer reads the value.
findings.recommendation: Delete the three `STUB_MARKER` consts and the three `const _:` assertions + their TODO comment in benten-engine/src/lib.rs lines 42-49. Removing dead markers is a trivial edit with no downstream impact — they are re-exported nowhere except the engine's three `const _:` lines.

findings.title: Crate-level `#![allow(...)]` missing `reason =` on napi bindings (3 lints silenced)
findings.description:
`#![allow(clippy::needless_pass_by_value, clippy::missing_safety_doc, clippy::useless_conversion)]` does NOT carry a `reason =` string, unlike nearly every other allow in the workspace. The preceding comment explains the context (`napi-rs generates code that triggers pedantic lints we don't control`) but the attribute itself has no machine-readable reason.

Note: workspace `Cargo.toml` sets `allow_attributes_without_reason = "allow"`, so this is not a clippy failure — it's a consistency gap.
findings.recommendation: Add `reason = "napi-rs v3 macro-expanded FFI bindings trip pedantic lints we do not control; silenced at crate root so our own code still benefits from pedantic"` to the crate-level attribute.

findings.title: `#[allow(dead_code)]` without reason on `_keep_imports_live`
findings.description: Lint silencer function has `#[allow(dead_code)]` with no `reason =`. Inconsistent with the surrounding file which uses reasons elsewhere (e.g. line 494).
findings.recommendation: Change to `#[allow(dead_code, reason = "lint silencer so conditional imports stay live")]`.

findings.title: Two lint-suppress attributes without `reason =`
findings.description: Two callsites elide the reason string. Both are easily explained (synthetic-panic test + test-only diag expectations), and matching the workspace convention makes grep-for-rationale consistent.
findings.recommendation: Add `reason = "..."` at each site. The trace.rs case can simply be folded into the sibling `#[allow(clippy::expect_used, reason = "test-only expectations")]` already used at mermaid.rs:92.

findings.title: EngineBuilder exposes both `with_test_ivm_budget` and `ivm_max_work_per_update` with identical semantics
findings.description:
```rust
#[must_use]
pub fn with_test_ivm_budget(mut self, b: u64) -> Self { self.test_ivm_budget = Some(b); self }

#[must_use]
pub fn ivm_max_work_per_update(mut self, n: u64) -> Self { self.test_ivm_budget = Some(n); self }
```
Both write the same field. Neither has a doc comment explaining when to use which. This is a discoverability trap for anyone hitting IvmViewStale — they grep the surface, find two setters, can't tell if they're independent.
findings.recommendation: Pick one (probably `ivm_max_work_per_update` as the user-facing name; the `with_test_` prefix is dev-only) and either remove the other or document it as an alias marked `#[doc(hidden)]`. Add a doc line explaining the relationship to the stale-view code path.

findings.title: Error enum derives not uniform — none derive `Clone`, which blocks FFI error-fan-out without allocation
findings.description:
Every `*Error` enum derives only `Debug, thiserror::Error`. ErrorCode (the stable discriminant) additionally derives `Clone, PartialEq, Eq`. When napi code turns a Rust error into a JS error object, it has to format the Display once and discard — fine today. But Phase 2 wants to attach the error to a trace step (where both the display string AND a typed error code may be needed), and not having `Clone` forces manual `match` ladders to reconstruct equivalents.

Not a bug; a minor consistency / future-proofing concern.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding `Clone` to the non-source-bearing variants in a follow-up (would need boxing of source errors that aren't themselves `Clone`). Flagging for R6 triage; no Phase-1 action required.

findings.title: lib.rs is 3151 lines — largest file in the workspace by a factor of 3x over the next-largest (benten-eval at 1099)
findings.description: Everything except the change-broadcast is inline in one file: EngineError (55 lines), AnchorHandle+Outcome+SubgraphSpec, Engine struct + 40+ impls, EngineBuilder, testing module, 150-line test module. G7 acknowledges in the header doc that this is a Phase-1 composition shape. Navigation cost is real: jumping from `pub fn create_node` to the matching `impl PrimitiveHost` takes a thoughtful grep.
findings.recommendation: Not a Phase-1 blocker. For Phase-2: extract `builder.rs`, `testing.rs`, `subgraph_spec.rs`, `engine_impl.rs` — target <1000 lines each. `change.rs` is already broken out so the pattern exists.

findings.title: STUB_MARKER naming inconsistency: `benten-eval::stub` vs `benten-caps::phase-1` vs `benten-ivm::phase-1`
findings.description: Already flagged for deletion in r6-cr-8; surfacing separately because if the markers survive deletion-pass review for any reason (e.g. tests pin on specific strings), at least normalize to one format.
findings.recommendation: If retained, pick one format and use it everywhere. Preferably delete all three per r6-cr-8.

findings.title: Integration-test file layout is inconsistent across crates — benten-engine uses `tests/integration/*.rs` wrapped in a `tests/integration.rs` aggregator, while all others flat-list integration tests in `tests/*.rs`
findings.description:
benten-engine's aggregator (`tests/integration.rs` with `mod integration { pub mod foo; ... }`) is a deliberate cost-optimization (single test-binary compile for 12 scenarios). The doc comment at `tests/integration.rs:1-10` explains this. Other crates with many tests (benten-core has 20+ integration files, benten-graph has 20+, benten-eval has 19) pay the per-file link cost — each file is its own test binary.

Quick measurement: `cargo test` workspace link time could drop measurably if other high-file-count crates adopted the same pattern.
findings.recommendation: Phase-2 perf pass: roll out the aggregator pattern to benten-core/tests, benten-graph/tests, benten-eval/tests. Not a Phase-1 blocker — it's a build-time win, not a correctness concern.

findings.title: Two `#[allow(clippy::todo)]` attributes on testing / test modules — redundant with workspace-level `todo = "allow"`
findings.description: Both `#[allow(clippy::todo, reason = "Phase-2 scope")]` (line 3004) on `pub mod testing` and the workspace already-allowed `clippy::todo` setting cancel out. No warning would fire either way.
findings.recommendation: Delete both `clippy::todo` entries from the attribute lists. Leave the `unwrap_used` / `expect_used` allows — those ARE load-bearing (workspace is `unwrap_used = allow` but a future tightening might make them warn).

positive_observations: Zero `todo!()` or `unimplemented!()` in any `src/**/*.rs` — the red-phase scaffolding has been cleanly removed.
Every error enum message is lowercase and contextual. Display-format uniformity across the 7 error types is unusually good.

`#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` on all 5 pure-Rust crates. `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` on the napi crate (necessary for the macro) with a clear explanatory comment — the ONLY place unsafe could enter the workspace.
`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` is clean without suppression — the workspace clippy policy is strict (pedantic on) and the per-crate carve-outs each carry documented reasons.
Capability layering is preserved: benten-caps has only a dev-dep on benten-graph (the broken doc link is the ONLY symptom of wanting more — and it's cosmetic).
Feature gate for `diag` is done correctly: stub modules present under `#[cfg(not(feature = "diag"))]` so the thin-engine path compiles without dead-signature headaches.
No `dbg!()` macro calls, no stray `println!` / `eprintln!` in library code except three documented Phase-1-placeholder-for-tracing sites (all with `#[allow(clippy::print_stderr, reason = ...)]`).
Test file naming is descriptive and consistent: `{primitive}_{concern}.rs` or `proptests_{subject}.rs` across all crates.
Cargo.toml workspace inheritance (version/edition/rust-version/license/authors/repository) is used by every crate — no accidental drift in those fields.

order: r6-cr-8 + r6-cr-15 (delete STUB_MARKER trio + the three const _: assertions — ~7 lines removed)
r6-cr-3, r6-cr-4, r6-cr-5, r6-cr-17 (delete stale `#![allow(clippy::todo)]` entries — ~8 lines removed)
r6-cr-6, r6-cr-7 (update stale doc-headers + two Cargo.toml descriptions — ~10 lines edited)
r6-cr-9, r6-cr-10, r6-cr-11 (add `reason =` to 4 remaining bare `#[allow]` sites — ~6 lines edited)
r6-cr-12 (collapse the two IVM-budget setters or doc the alias)
r6-cr-2 (doc the 125 undocumented pub items — OR defer to R6 triage as a Phase-2 polish item)
